name: Pattanaik 2019 Tadalafil ED Endothelial Dysfunction RCT Extract
population_or_context: Men with erectile dysfunction and brachial artery flow-mediated dilation below 15%.
methods: Double-blind randomized placebo-controlled 4-week trial comparing low-dose tadalafil with placebo; endpoints were IIEF-5 and brachial artery flow-mediated dilation measured by blinded observer.
population_profile: Men with ED and endothelial dysfunction; not a healthy off-label population.
tadalafil_exposure: Low-dose tadalafil for 4 weeks; abstract does not specify exact dose in the displayed extract.
safety_findings: Adverse events were significantly more frequent with tadalafil than placebo in the abstract.
directness_to_target_population: Low to moderate for men with ED/endothelial dysfunction; low for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Large placebo response, short duration, ED population, abstract-level dose ambiguity, and no clinical cardiovascular outcomes.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; transfer limit is population, short follow-up, and placebo-like FMD response.
